beats.activity: Wake up with coffee on the porch, enjoying the calm Sunday morning.
beats.reasoning: Grace starts her Sundays on the porch with coffee—a ritual that grounds her. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means she'll linger at home longer than usual. Perfect weather invites outdoor time.

beats.activity: Leisurely breakfast and journaling at home, reviewing film camera shots from the week.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual; Grace is still low-energy and prefers to ease into the day. Film photography is a constant companion, and she'll review recent rolls before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill for a Sunday walk and farmer's market visit.
beats.reasoning: Across-town drive from Redmond to Capitol Hill area. Grace loves the farmers market on Sundays, it's a quiet ritual before the week. 30-min buffer accounts for light Sunday traffic.

beats.activity: Browse farmers market, grab fresh produce, snap some film photos of vendors and flowers.
beats.reasoning: Grace values tactile, organized places and loves Pike Place. The sunny weather and quiet Sunday morning make this an ideal time to wander and photograph. Aligns with her love of detail and beauty.

beats.activity: Walk to a nearby Capitol Hill café for a light brunch.
beats.reasoning: Grace frequents Molly Moon's—it's her go-to spot. Walking errand within Capitol Hill; she'll take photos of the neighborhood on the way. Keeps the day slow and intentional.

beats.activity: Sit at café with brunch, people-watch, and read a book.
beats.reasoning: Low energy means Grace will linger over a leisurely meal. The sunny weather and calm Sunday vibe align with her mood. She values quiet moments over crowds.

beats.activity: Walk through Capitol Hill neighborhood, taking film photos of storefronts and street scenes.
beats.reasoning: Grace carries her film camera everywhere and loves documenting the neighborhood. Sunny 72F weather is perfect for a walk. No major time pressure on a Sunday.

beats.activity: Drive to Gas Works Park for an afternoon sit and observation.
beats.reasoning: Grace loves this spot for its views and quiet reflection time. Short drive from Capitol Hill. She's drawn to parks and open space, especially on beautiful days like this.

beats.activity: Sit at Gas Works Park, watch the boats on Lake Union, take film photos of the water and skyline.
beats.reasoning: Perfect Sunday afternoon activity. Lake Union has heavy recreational boat traffic today—Grace will enjoy watching and photographing the activity. Aligns with her love of organized observation and calm contemplation.

beats.activity: Drive home to Redmond, stopping at Whole Foods to pick up dinner ingredients.
beats.reasoning: Across-town drive with a quick errand stop on the way. Grace values organized meal planning; she'll pick up fresh items from the market near her home. 45-min buffer for drive and shopping.

beats.activity: Prepare a simple dinner at home, eat on the porch while watching the sunset.
beats.reasoning: Grace ends Sundays with simple, intentional meals at home. The porch is her sanctuary. Sunset time around 9pm in June makes this the perfect wind-down moment.

diary:
Sunday mornings are sacred—I had my coffee on the porch with the whole day stretching ahead. Journaled a bit, looked over some film shots from the week. Nothing pressing, which felt like a luxury.

Drove into the city around ten and wandered Pike Place. The farmers market was busy but pleasant; I filled my bag with strawberries and picked up some beautiful dahlias. Used half a roll of film on the vendors—there's something about the morning light hitting those flower stands that never gets old.

Brunch at Molly Moon's turned into a two-hour affair. I read, watched people navigate Capitol Hill's Sunday rhythm, didn't rush anything. Walked through the neighborhood after, caught some good storefronts on film.

Gas Works in the afternoon was the real gift. Lake Union was alive with boats and paddleboards, sun hitting the water just right. I sat there for nearly two hours, camera in hand, mostly just watching. Felt grounded in a way that's rare.
thought_process: Today was one of those rare Sundays where I had no obligations pulling at me, and I leaned into that completely. The weather forecast—calm, sunny, 72—basically gave me permission to be aimless, and I took it. I chose the farmer's market because it feeds both my practical side (I needed fresh food) and my creative one (the light, the textures, the people). The extra time at Molly Moon's surprised me; I wasn't planning to linger that long, but I felt no pressure to move on. Gas Works was deliberate—I needed to sit with the water and the city and let my mind settle. No world drama today meant I could be entirely present in small, sensory moments. It's the kind of Sunday that reminds me why I love this city.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
